hero.subheadline: QSave syncs your save files to your own Google Drive. 19,000+ games supported across Steam, GOG, Epic, and Battle.net. Free and open source.

features.heading: Everything you need, nothing you don't
features.subheading: QSave stays out of your way. Install it, sign in to Google Drive, and back up your saves in one click.

search.title: Find your games instantly
search.description: Search across your installed games and the 19,000+ titles in our detection database. Filter by platform — Steam, GOG, Epic, Battle.net.
search.alt: QSave search screen showing game results

restore.title: Restore anywhere, anytime
restore.description: Pick any backup from your Drive and restore it in one click. Cross-platform path remapping keeps your saves portable between computers.

manual.title: Track the games we don't know yet
manual.description: Add any game manually by pointing QSave at its save folder. Your custom games sync alongside the detected ones.

free.question: Is QSave free?
free.answer: Yes — QSave is free and open source. The code is on GitHub and you can inspect or modify it.

storage.question: Where are my save files stored?
storage.answer: In your own Google Drive, inside a dedicated QSave folder. QSave only has access to files it created in that folder — it cannot see or touch anything else in your Drive. You control the data, and we never see or store your saves on any server.

os.question: Which operating systems are supported?
os.answer: Windows 10/11 and macOS (both Apple Silicon and Intel). Linux builds aren't distributed yet.

background.question: Does QSave run in the background?
background.answer: Optionally. You can launch QSave only when you want to back up, or set it to start with your system so it's always ready. Either way, QSave never backs up automatically — you choose when to back up.

signing.question: Why does my computer warn me when I open the installer?
signing.intro: Windows SmartScreen and macOS Gatekeeper show a warning because QSave isn't yet signed with a paid code-signing certificate. The app itself is safe — the warning is just your OS saying "I don't recognize this publisher yet."
signing.macos: macOS: Try to open the app first (it will be blocked), then go to System Settings → Privacy & Security, scroll to the Security section and click Open Anyway. You'll need to enter your password.
signing.outro: If you'd like to help us buy code-signing certificates so these warnings go away, see the Support section below.

uninstall.question: How do I uninstall QSave?
uninstall.answer: Uninstall like any other app: on Windows, use Settings → Apps; on macOS, drag QSave from Applications to the Trash. Your backups remain in your Google Drive until you delete them yourself.

support.heading: Help remove the scary warning
support.body: Windows and macOS show a warning when you install QSave because code-signing certificates cost money every year. Contributions on Ko-fi go directly toward buying them, so future installs are smooth for everyone.

footer.tagline: QSave is free and open source. Your saves stay in your Google Drive.
